# --- HELPER FUNCTIONS ---

# ==============================


# --- MODEL FUNCTIONS ---
# --- MODEL: OLS ---
# Define Ordinary Least Squares Model 'run_ols_model' function.
def run_ols_model(y, X, conf_level, sigma_value):
    model = sm.OLS(y, X).fit()
    influence = model.get_influence()
    alpha = 1 - (conf_level / 100)
    predictions = model.get_prediction(X).summary_frame(alpha=alpha)

    return {
        "model": model,
        "resid": model.resid,
        "fitted": model.fittedvalues,
        "std_resid": influence.resid_studentized_internal,
        "is_outlier": np.abs(influence.resid_studentized_internal) > sigma_value,
        "mean_se": predictions["mean_se"],
        "ci_lower": predictions["mean_ci_lower"],
        "ci_upper": predictions["mean_ci_upper"],
        "pi_lower": predictions["obs_ci_lower"],
        "pi_upper": predictions["obs_ci_upper"],
        "cooks_d": influence.cooks_distance[0],
        "leverage": influence.hat_matrix_diag,
        "summary": model.summary2().tables,
    }


# ==============================


# --- MODEL: WLS ---
# Define Weighted Least Squares Model 'run_wls_model' function.
def run_wls_model(y, X, wls_weight, x_raw, conf_level, sigma_value):
    weights = {
        "none": np.ones(len(y)),
        "n_obs": len(y),
        "inv_x": 1 / x_raw,
        "inv_x2": 1 / x_raw**2,
        "inv_sqrt_x": 1 / np.sqrt(x_raw),
        "uniform_random": np.random.rand(len(y)),
    }.get(wls_weight, np.ones(len(y)))
    if wls_weight == "inv_var_x":
        weights = 1 / (sm.OLS(y, X).fit().resid ** 2 + 1e-5)

    model = sm.WLS(y, X, weights=weights).fit()
    alpha = 1 - (conf_level / 100)
    predictions = model.get_prediction(X).summary_frame(alpha=alpha)
    # Influence for WLS uses whitened data
    whitened_model = sm.OLS(model.model.wendog, model.model.wexog).fit()
    influence = OLSInfluence(whitened_model)

    return {
        "model": model,
        "resid": model.wresid,  # Whitened residuals
        "fitted": model.fittedvalues,
        "std_resid": influence.resid_studentized_internal,
        "is_outlier": np.abs(influence.resid_studentized_internal) > sigma_value,
        "mean_se": predictions["mean_se"],
        "ci_lower": predictions["mean_ci_lower"],
        "ci_upper": predictions["mean_ci_upper"],
        "pi_lower": predictions["obs_ci_lower"],
        "pi_upper": predictions["obs_ci_upper"],
        "cooks_d": influence.cooks_distance[0],
        "leverage": influence.hat_matrix_diag,
        "summary": model.summary2().tables,
    }


# ==============================


# --- MODEL: GLS ---
# Define Generalized Least Squares Model 'run_gls_model' function.
def run_gls_model(y, X, auto_rho, rho_val, conf_level, sigma_value):
    if auto_rho is False:
        rho = rho_val
    else:
        ols_resid = sm.OLS(y, X).fit().resid
        rho = np.corrcoef(ols_resid[:-1], ols_resid[1:])[0, 1]

    sigma = toeplitz(np.power(rho, np.arange(len(y))))
    model = sm.GLS(y, X, sigma=sigma).fit()
    alpha = 1 - (conf_level / 100)
    predictions = model.get_prediction(X).summary_frame(alpha=alpha)
    whitened_model = sm.OLS(model.model.wendog, model.model.wexog).fit()
    influence = OLSInfluence(whitened_model)

    return {
        "model": model,
        "resid": model.wresid,
        "fitted": model.fittedvalues,
        "std_resid": influence.resid_studentized_internal,
        "is_outlier": np.abs(influence.resid_studentized_internal) > sigma_value,
        # "mean_se": np.sqrt(model.mse_resid),
        # "fit": predictions["mean"],
        "mean_se": predictions["mean_se"],
        "ci_lower": predictions["mean_ci_lower"],
        "ci_upper": predictions["mean_ci_upper"],
        "pi_lower": predictions["obs_ci_lower"],
        "pi_upper": predictions["obs_ci_upper"],
        "cooks_d": influence.cooks_distance[0],
        "leverage": influence.hat_matrix_diag,
        "summary": model.summary2().tables,
    }


# ==============================


# --- MODEL: ReLS ---
# Define Recursive Least Squares Model 'run_rels_model' function.
def run_rels_model(y, X, conf_level, sigma_value):
    model = sm.RecursiveLS(y, X).fit()
    std_r = model.resid / np.nanstd(model.resid)

    # Confidence and Predictive Intervals
    # RecursiveLS summary doesn't provide standard interval frames like OLS
    params = model.params
    # Calculating mean_se, ci_lower, ci_upper, pi_lower, pi_upper
    interval_results = []
    for i in range(5, len(y) + 1):
        ols_model = sm.OLS(y[:i], X[:i]).fit()
        # Predict for the current (latest) observation in the window
        current_X = X.iloc[i - 1 : i]
        predictions = ols_model.get_prediction(current_X).summary_frame(
            alpha=1 - (conf_level / 100)
        )

        # Extract the point prediction
        fitted_val = predictions["mean"].iloc[0]

        # Manually calculate the residual: Actual - Fitted
        actual_val = y.iloc[i - 1]
        residual_val = actual_val - fitted_val

        # Convert results into pd.Series()
        interval_series = pd.Series(
            {
                "observation": i,
                "fit": fitted_val,
                "residual": residual_val,
                "mean_se": predictions["mean_se"].iloc[0],
                "ci_lower": predictions["mean_ci_lower"].iloc[0],
                "ci_upper": predictions["mean_ci_upper"].iloc[0],
                "pi_lower": predictions["obs_ci_lower"].iloc[0],
                "pi_upper": predictions["obs_ci_upper"].iloc[0],
            }
        )

        interval_results.append(interval_series)

    # Calculating Cook's Distance.
    # 1. Get Recursive Residuals (Standardized)
    # r_t: normalized prediction errors
    r_t = model.resid_recursive

    # 2. Extract Leverage (h_t)
    # For RLS, leverage is calculated from the filtered covariance matrices
    # h_t = x_t' P_{t-1} x_t
    k = model.model.k_exog
    n = len(r_t)
    h_t = np.zeros(n)

    # Accessing the filtered covariance P_t
    # Note: res.filtered_state_cov provides P_t
    # Calculate leverage using the exogenous matrix from the model
    for t in range(k, n):
        # Correctly pull the row from model.exog
        x_t = model.model.exog[t, :]
        # p_prev is the filtered state covariance from the previous step
        p_prev = model.filtered_state_cov[:, :, t - 1]
        # Recursive leverage formula: x_t' * P_{t-1} * x_t
        h_t[t] = x_t.T @ p_prev @ x_t

    # 3. Calculate Cook's Distance
    # s2 is the recursive mean squared error
    s2 = np.var(r_t[k:])
    # Formula: (r_t^2 / (k * s2)) * (h_t / (1 - h_t)^2)
    cooks_d = (r_t**2 / (k * s2)) * (h_t / (1 - h_t) ** 2)

    return {
        "model": model,
        "resid": model.resid,
        "fitted": model.fittedvalues,
        "std_resid": std_r,
        "is_outlier": np.abs(std_r) > sigma_value,
        "interval_results": pd.DataFrame(interval_results),
        "cooks_d": cooks_d,
        "params_evolution": params,
        "cusum": model.cusum,
        "summary": model.summary().tables,
    }


# ==============================


# --- MODEL: RoLS ---
# Define Generalized Least Squares Model 'run_rols_model' function.
def run_rols_model(y, X, window_size, conf_level, sigma_value):
    model = RollingOLS(y, X, window=window_size).fit()
    # Rolling doesn't have standard residuals in the same way; we calculate manually
    params = model.params
    fitted = (X * params).sum(axis=1)
    resid = y - fitted
    std_r = (resid / np.nanstd(resid)).values  # calculate standard residuals

    # Generate the complete confidence interval DataFrame over time
    # By default, alpha=0.05 constructs a 95% interval ([0.025, 0.975])
    ci_df = model.conf_int(alpha=0.05)
    # Extract lower [0.025] and upper [0.975] boundaries
    # Using .xs allows us to slice into the complex MultiIndex cleanly
    lower_ci = ci_df.xs("lower", axis=1, level=1)
    upper_ci = ci_df.xs("upper", axis=1, level=1)

    # Calculating mean_se, ci_lower, ci_upper, pi_lower, pi_upper, and cooks_d
    interval_results = []
    cooks_d = []
    for i in range(window_size, len(y)):
        ols_model = sm.OLS(y[i - window_size : i], X[i - window_size : i]).fit()
        # Predict for the current (latest) observation in the window
        current_X = X.iloc[i - 1 : i]
        predictions = ols_model.get_prediction(current_X).summary_frame(
            alpha=1 - (conf_level / 100)
        )

        # Extract the point prediction
        fitted_val = predictions["mean"].iloc[0]

        # Manually calculate the residual: Actual - Fitted
        actual_val = y.iloc[i - 1]
        residual_val = actual_val - fitted_val

        interval_results.append(
            {
                "observation": i,
                "fit": fitted_val,
                "residual": residual_val,
                "mean_se": predictions["mean_se"].iloc[0],
                "ci_lower": predictions["mean_ci_lower"].iloc[0],
                "ci_upper": predictions["mean_ci_upper"].iloc[0],
                "pi_lower": predictions["obs_ci_lower"].iloc[0],
                "pi_upper": predictions["obs_ci_upper"].iloc[0],
            }
        )
        influence = ols_model.get_influence()
        # Get Cook's D for the LAST observation in this window
        cooks_d.append(influence.cooks_distance[0][-1])

    return {
        "model": model,
        "resid": resid,
        "fitted": fitted,
        "std_resid": std_r,
        "is_outlier": np.abs(std_r) > sigma_value,
        "params_evolution": params,
        "std_errors": model.bse,
        "t_values": model.tvalues,
        "p_values": model.pvalues,
        "ci_df": ci_df,
        "lower_ci": lower_ci,
        "upper_ci": upper_ci,
        "observation": [i["observation"] for i in interval_results],
        "fit": [i["fit"] for i in interval_results],
        "residual": [i["residual"] for i in interval_results],
        "mean_se": [i["mean_se"] for i in interval_results],
        "ci_lower": [i["ci_lower"] for i in interval_results],
        "ci_upper": [i["ci_upper"] for i in interval_results],
        "pi_lower": [i["pi_lower"] for i in interval_results],
        "pi_upper": [i["pi_upper"] for i in interval_results],
        "cooks_d": cooks_d,
        "summary": [
            pd.DataFrame(
                {
                    "Note": [
                        "Rolling OLS Summary not available in table format--see plots"
                    ]
                }
            )
        ],
    }

# --- SERVER LOGIC ---
# Server Function
def server(input, output, session):
    current_page = reactive.Value(0)
    # ==============================

    # Theme
    @reactive.calc
    def active_theme():
        # This only runs once per toggle, even with 10 plots
        # If the theme is changed, the plots will be updated
        # If the theme is 'light', use the light theme. Otherwise, use the dark theme
        # Inheret the light/dark mode from the Quarto dashboard theme settings.
        # If the theme is 'light', use the light theme. Otherwise, use the dark theme

        return THEME_CONFIG[input.dark_mode()]

    # ==============================

    # 1. Main Analysis Panel
    # Data
    # Define 'data' reactive expression.
    # Data is read from the uploaded file or a local file.
    # Returns the "Boston.csv" pandas DataFrame unless a file is uploaded.
    @reactive.calc
    def data():
        f = input.file1()
        if f:
            return pd.read_csv(f[0]["datapath"])

        local_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "Boston.csv")

        if os.path.exists(local_path):
            return pd.read_csv(local_path)

        return pd.DataFrame()

    # ==============================

    # Define '_update_cols' reactive expression.
    # Update 'x_col' and 'y_col' select widgets
    # When 'data' reactive expression changes, update 'x_col' and 'y_col' select widgets.
    @reactive.effect
    def _update_cols():
        df = data()
        if df is not None and not df.empty:
            cols = df.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns.tolist()
            ui.update_select("x_col", choices=cols, selected=cols[6] if cols else None)
            ui.update_select(
                "y_col", choices=cols, selected=cols[7] if len(cols) > 1 else None
            )

    # ==============================

    # Define Regression Parameters 'get_regression_params' reactive expression.
    @reactive.calc
    def get_regression_params():
        # Halt execution if inputs are not selected
        req(input.x_col.get(), input.y_col.get())
        # Get data.
        df = data()

        x_col = input.x_col()
        y_col = input.y_col()

        df_reg = df[[x_col, y_col]].apply(pd.to_numeric, errors="coerce").dropna()

        # Sort df_reg by x_col
        df_reg = df_reg.sort_values(by=x_col).reset_index(drop=True)

        # Add constant
        X = sm.add_constant(df_reg[x_col])
        # Get y
        y = df_reg[y_col]

        return {"df_reg": df_reg, "X": X, "y": y, "x_col": x_col, "y_col": y_col}

    # ==============================

    # Define 'model_results' reactive expression.
    # Get model results based on user input selection (OLS, WLS, GLS, ReLS, or RoLS).
    @reactive.calc
    def model_results():
        # Get model type
        model_type = input.model_type()

        # Get Regression Parameters ('df_reg', 'X', 'y', x_col, y_col)
        regression_params = get_regression_params()
        df_reg = regression_params["df_reg"]
        X = regression_params["X"]
        y = regression_params["y"]
        x_col = regression_params["x_col"]
        regression_params["y_col"]

        # Get Confidence Level
        conf_level = input.conf_level()

        # Get Sigma Value
        sigma_value = input.sigma_value()

        # If model type is OLS
        if model_type == "OLS":
            return run_ols_model(y, X, conf_level, sigma_value)

        # If model type is WLS
        if model_type == "WLS":
            return run_wls_model(
                y, X, input.wls_weight(), df_reg[x_col], conf_level, sigma_value
            )
        # If model type is GLS
        if model_type == "GLS":
            return run_gls_model(
                y, X, input.auto_rho(), input.rho_val(), conf_level, sigma_value
            )
        # If model type is ReLS
        if model_type == "ReLS":
            return run_rels_model(y, X, conf_level, sigma_value)
        # If model type is RoLS
        if model_type == "RoLS":
            return run_rols_model(y, X, input.window_size(), conf_level, sigma_value)

        # ==============================

    # Define 'equation' reactive expression.
    # Generate linear regression equation
    @render.text
    def equation():
        # Get model type
        model_type = input.model_type()

        # Get Regression Parameters ('df_reg', 'X', 'y', x_col, y_col)
        regression_params = get_regression_params()
        regression_params["df_reg"]
        regression_params["X"]
        regression_params["y"]
        x_col = regression_params["x_col"]
        y_col = regression_params["y_col"]

        res = model_results()
        if not res:
            return "Awaiting Data..."

        # If model type is GLS
        if model_type == "GLS":
            return f"Line Equation: {y_col} = ({res['model'].params.iloc[1]:.4f} * {x_col}) + ({res['model'].params.iloc[0]:.4f})"

        # If model type is ReLS
        elif model_type == "ReLS":
            return f"Line Equation: {y_col} = ({res['model'].params.iloc[1]:.4f} * {x_col}) + ({res['model'].params.iloc[0]:.4f})"

        # If model type is Rols
        elif model_type == "RoLS":
            return "Line Equation: Time-varying parameters (see plot)"

        # If model type is OLS or WLS
        p = res["model"].params
        return (
            f"Line Equation: {y_col} = ({p.iloc[1]:.4f} * {x_col}) + ({p.iloc[0]:.4f})"
        )

        # ==============================

    # 1. Main Regression Plot.
    # Define 'plot_fit_interactive' reactive expression.
    # Generate interactive plot
    @render_widget
    def plot_fit_interactive():
        # Get the current mode ('light' or 'dark')
        theme = active_theme()

        # Get model type
        model_type = input.model_type()

        # Get Regression Parameters ('df_reg', 'X', 'y', x_col, y_col)
        regression_params = get_regression_params()
        df_reg = regression_params["df_reg"]
        regression_params["X"]
        y = regression_params["y"]
        x_col = regression_params["x_col"]
        y_col = regression_params["y_col"]

        # Get Confidence Level
        conf_level = input.conf_level()

        # Get Sigma Value
        input.sigma_value()

        # Get the model results
        res = model_results()  # May need to use model.fit()...?
        if not res:
            return go.Figure()

        # Initialize figure
        fig = go.Figure()

        # Plot Data Points
        fig.add_trace(
            go.Scatter(
                x=df_reg[x_col],
                y=y,
                mode="markers",
                name="Data",
                marker=dict(color=theme["font"], opacity=0.8),
            )
        )
        # Plot Fit Line.
        # Plot regression line
        fig.add_trace(
            go.Scatter(
                x=df_reg[x_col],
                y=res["fitted"],
                mode="lines",
                name=f"{input.model_type()} Fit",
                line=dict(color=theme["info"]),
            )
        )
        # Plot CI Ribbon
        if "ci_lower" in res and "ci_upper" in res:
            fig.add_trace(
                go.Scatter(
                    x=np.concatenate([df_reg[x_col], df_reg[x_col][::-1]]),
                    y=np.concatenate(
                        [
                            res["ci_upper"],
                            res["ci_lower"][::-1],
                        ]
                    ),
                    fill="toself",
                    fillcolor=theme["stroke-opacity-0.3"],
                    line=dict(color=theme["stroke-opacity-0.4"]),
                    name=f"{conf_level}% Conf. Interval",
                )
            )
        # Plot PI Ribbon
        if "pi_lower" in res and "pi_upper" in res:
            fig.add_trace(
                go.Scatter(
                    x=np.concatenate([df_reg[x_col], df_reg[x_col][::-1]]),
                    y=np.concatenate([res["pi_upper"], res["pi_lower"][::-1]]),
                    mode="lines",
                    fill="toself",
                    fillcolor=theme["stroke-opacity-0.1"],
                    line=dict(color=theme["stroke-opacity-0.4"]),
                    name=f"{conf_level}% Preds. Interval",
                )
            )
        # Plot Outliers
        if res["is_outlier"] is not None and not res["is_outlier"].size == 0:
            is_outlier = res["is_outlier"]
            outliers = df_reg[is_outlier]
            fig.add_trace(
                go.Scatter(
                    x=outliers[x_col],
                    y=outliers[y_col],
                    mode="markers+text",
                    text=[f"Obs {i}" for i in np.arange(len(outliers))],
                    textposition="top center",
                    marker=dict(color=theme["fill"], size=10, symbol="circle-open"),
                    name="Outliers",
                )
            )
        # Update layout
        fig.update_layout(
            title=f"Regression ({model_type}) Fit with Confidence and Prediction Intervals & Outliers",
            xaxis_title=x_col,
            yaxis_title=y_col,
            hovermode="closest",
        )

        return apply_plot_theme(fig, theme)

        # ==============================

    # "Model Error Metrics" table.
    # Define 'table_metrics' reactive expression.
    @render.table
    def table_metrics():
        # Get model type
        input.model_type()

        # Get Regression Parameters ('df_reg', 'X', 'y', x_col, y_col)
        regression_params = get_regression_params()
        regression_params["df_reg"]
        regression_params["X"]
        y = regression_params["y"]
        regression_params["x_col"]
        regression_params["y_col"]

        # Get Confidence Level
        input.conf_level()

        # Get Sigma Value
        input.sigma_value()

        # Get the model results
        res = model_results()
        if not res:
            return None

        # Get Model Residual Statistics
        try:
            r = res["resid"]
            return pd.DataFrame(
                {
                    "Metric": ["MSE", "RMSE", "NRMSE", "MAE"],
                    "Value": [
                        f"{np.mean(r**2):.4f}",
                        f"{np.sqrt(np.mean(r**2)):.4f}",
                        f"{np.sqrt(np.mean(r**2)) / np.std(y):.4f}",
                        f"{np.mean(np.abs(r)):.4f}",
                    ],
                }
            )
        except Exception:
            return pd.DataFrame({"Notice": ["Model Error Metrics table unavailable"]})

        # ==============================

    # "Multicollinearity (VIF)" table.
    # Define 'table_vif' reactive expression.
    @render.table
    def table_vif():
        # Get Regression Parameters ('df_reg', 'X', 'y', x_col, y_col)
        regression_params = get_regression_params()
        regression_params["df_reg"]
        X = regression_params["X"]
        regression_params["y"]
        regression_params["x_col"]
        regression_params["y_col"]
        # Get the model results
        res = model_results()
        if not res:
            return None

        # Get Variance Inflation Factors
        X_v = X.values
        return pd.DataFrame(
            {
                "Variable": X.columns,
                "VIF": [f"{vif(X_v, i):.4f}" for i in range(X_v.shape[1])],
            }
        )

        # ==============================

    # Define 'extract_table' reactive expression.
    def extract_table(idx):
        # Get model type
        model_type = input.model_type()

        # Get the Window Size
        window_size = input.window_size()

        # Get the model results
        res = model_results()
        if not res:
            return None

        if model_type == "RoLS":
            if idx == 0:
                return pd.DataFrame({"Info": ["Rolling OLS Summary"]})
            if idx == 1:
                return pd.DataFrame(
                    {
                        "Variable": res["params_evolution"].columns,
                        "Coef": res["params_evolution"].iloc[window_size - 1].round(4),
                        "std err": res["std_errors"].iloc[window_size - 1].round(3),
                        "t": res["t_values"].iloc[window_size - 1].round(3),
                        "P>|t|": res["p_values"][window_size - 1].round(3),
                        "[0.025": res["lower_ci"].iloc[window_size - 1].round(3),
                        "0.975]": res["upper_ci"].iloc[window_size - 1].round(3),
                    }
                )
            return pd.DataFrame(
                {"Metric": ["Window Size"], "Value": [input.window_size()]}
            )

        summ = res["model"].summary()
        if idx < len(summ.tables):
            # Access the raw string matrix inside the statsmodels SimpleTable
            raw_data = summ.tables[idx].data

            # Strip invisible padding spaces from cells
            cleaned_rows = [[str(cell).strip() for cell in row] for row in raw_data]

            # 1. Build DataFrame with sequential temporary names to prevent column clashes
            df = pd.DataFrame(cleaned_rows[1:])

            # 2. Safely drop empty rows and columns by positional values, not names
            df = df.loc[:, (df != "").any(axis=0)]
            df = df[~(df == "").all(axis=1)]

            # 3. Assign the actual cleaned headers back onto the deduplicated frame
            header_row = cleaned_rows[0][: len(df.columns)]
            df.columns = header_row

            return df
        return pd.DataFrame()

    # "Model Information" table ("table_summary_0").
    # Define 'table_summary_0' reactive expression.
    @render.table(index=False)
    def table_summary_0():
        try:
            # Get the model results
            return extract_table(0)

        except Exception:
            return pd.DataFrame({"Notice": ["Model Information table unavailable"]})

    # Define 'add_stars' function.
    # Add stars to p-values (column 'P>|t|' in 'table_summary_1').
    def add_stars(p):
        if p < 0.001:
            return "***"
        if p < 0.01:
            return "**"
        if p < 0.05:
            return "*"
        if p < 0.1:
            return "."
        return ""

    # Confidence and Predictive Intervals ("table_intervals") Output Table
    # Define 'table_intervals' reactive expression.
    @render.table
    def table_intervals():
        # Get model type
        model_type = input.model_type()

        # Get Regression Parameters ('df_reg', 'X', 'y', x_col, y_col)
        regression_params = get_regression_params()

        input.sigma_value()

        # Get Window Size
        window_size = input.window_size()

        # Get the model results
        res = model_results()
        if not res:
            return None

        try:
            if model_type == "ReLS":
                # Start at index 4 for x_col and y_col
                x_col_sliced = df_reg[x_col].iloc[4:].reset_index(drop=True)
                y_col_sliced = df_reg[y_col].iloc[4:].reset_index(drop=True)
                table_intervals_df = pd.DataFrame(
                    {
                        x_col: x_col_sliced,
                        y_col: y_col_sliced,
                        "Mean": res["interval_results"]["fit"],
                        "Residual": res["interval_results"]["residual"],
                        "Mean SE": res["interval_results"]["mean_se"],
                        "CI (lower)": res["interval_results"]["ci_lower"],
                        "CI (upper)": res["interval_results"]["ci_upper"],
                        "PI (lower)": res["interval_results"]["pi_lower"],
                        "PI (upper)": res["interval_results"]["pi_upper"],
                    }
                )

            elif model_type == "RoLS":
                # Start at index "window_size" for x_col and y_col
                x_col_sliced = df_reg[x_col].iloc[window_size:].reset_index(drop=True)
                y_col_sliced = df_reg[y_col].iloc[window_size:].reset_index(drop=True)
                table_intervals_df = pd.DataFrame(
                    {
                        x_col: x_col_sliced,
                        y_col: y_col_sliced,
                        "Mean": res["fit"],
                        "Residual": res["residual"],
                        "Mean SE": res["mean_se"],
                        "CI (lower)": res["ci_lower"],
                        "CI (upper)": res["ci_upper"],
                        "PI (lower)": res["pi_lower"],
                        "PI (upper)": res["pi_upper"],
                    }
                )

            else:
                # Return Confidence and Predictive Intervals from the model
                table_intervals_df = pd.concat(
                    {
                        x_col: df_reg[x_col],
                        y_col: df_reg[y_col],
                        "Mean": res["fitted"],
                        "Residual": res["resid"],
                        "Mean SE": res["mean_se"],
                        "CI (lower)": res["ci_lower"],
                        "CI (upper)": res["ci_upper"],
                        "PI (lower)": res["pi_lower"],
                        "PI (upper)": res["pi_upper"],
                    },
                    axis=1,
                )

            start = current_page.get() * 10

            return table_intervals_df.iloc[start : start + 10]

        except Exception:
            return pd.DataFrame({"Notice": ["Interval table unavailable"]})

            return go.Figure()

        colors = [
            theme["fill"] if outlier else theme["font"] for outlier in res["is_outlier"]
        ]

        # Add scatter plot

                x=res["fitted"],
                y=res["resid"],
                mode="markers",
                marker=dict(color=colors),

        )

        # Add horizontal line
        fig.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dash", line_color=theme["info"])

        # Update layout

            xaxis_title="Fitted Values",
            yaxis_title="Residuals",
            title="Residuals vs Fitted Values",
        )

        return apply_plot_theme(fig, theme)

            fig.add_trace(

            fig.add_trace(

            fig.update_layout(

                xaxis_title="Observation Index",
                yaxis_title="CUSUM",

        if model_type == "RoLS":
            # Plot Parameter Evolution

                    y=pd.concat(

                    fill="toself",
                    fillcolor=theme["stroke-opacity-0.3"],
                    line_color=theme["stroke-opacity-0.4"],
                    hoverinfo="skip",
                    showlegend=False,
                    name="Confidence Interval",

            )
            # 2. Add the Predicted Mean Line
            fig.add_trace(
                go.Scatter(
                    x=res["observation"],
                    y=res["fit"],
                    line_color=theme["font"],
                    name="Predicted Mean",
                )

            # 3. Add the True Mean Line
            fig.add_trace(
                go.Scatter(
                    x=res["observation"],
                    y=res["fitted"],
                    line_color=theme["info"],
                    name="True Mean",
                )
            )
            # 4. Update the layout
            fig.update_layout(
                title="Parameter Evolution",
                xaxis_title="Observation Index",
                yaxis_title="Parameter Value",
                yaxis=dict(nticks=10),
                hovermode="x unified",
            )

        else:
            fig.update_layout(
                title="Plot not supported for static (OLS, WLS, GLS) models"
            )

        # Return figure
        return apply_plot_theme(fig, theme)

    # 10. Correlation Heatmap Pane
    # Define 'plot_heatmap' reactive expression.

    @render_widget

        fig = px.imshow(

        fig.update_layout(

            xaxis_title="Variables",

        return apply_plot_theme(fig, theme)
